title: Indulgent Pan-Seared Algerian Chervil Tagine
description: A flavorful, quick-fix Algerian dish roasted to perfection, featuring chervil, confit-tomato, and a hint of spices, served in an iron teppan pan.
instructions: Heat the iron teppan pan over medium-high heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of olive oil and swirl to coat the pan.
Add the chervil and cook until wilted.
Add the confit tomatoes, preserved lemon, paprika, cumin, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Transfer the mixture to a plate.
Wipe the pan clean and add the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil.
Return the pan to the stovetop and reduce heat to medium.
Add the parsley and cook until slightly wilted.
Return the chervil mixture to the pan and stir to combine.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
